source_file: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

heuristics.rule: Start planning Plan B after the first H-1B miss, not after the third
heuristics.condition: When you are on STEM OPT and have not yet won the H-1B lottery
heuristics.action: Begin researching and preparing alternative visa/immigration paths (Canada PR, O-1, NIW, cap-exempt employers) in parallel with the next lottery attempt
heuristics.because: The three-miss probability is 25-30%, which is high enough to treat as a realistic scenario rather than an edge case; waiting until after all three attempts wastes years of preparation time

heuristics.rule: Pursue cap-exempt employer structures if you cannot win the H-1B lottery
heuristics.condition: When a candidate has failed H-1B lottery multiple times but wants to remain in the US
heuristics.action: Seek employment at universities, nonprofit research institutions, or government research agencies, which can sponsor H-1B at any time without lottery
heuristics.because: These employer categories are explicitly exempt from the H-1B annual cap, removing the lottery dependency entirely

heuristics.rule: Use EB-2 NIW as a self-petition path for STEM graduates who cannot get employer sponsorship
heuristics.condition: When a STEM-background applicant lacks a sponsoring employer or has lost H-1B lottery multiple times
heuristics.action: Evaluate eligibility under the Matter of Dhanasar three-prong test and file EB-2 NIW self-petition
heuristics.because: NIW does not require employer sponsorship, and the community notes the threshold is lower than many assume for STEM candidates

decision_factors.factor: Cap-exempt employer availability in your field
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Working for a cap-exempt employer eliminates lottery dependency entirely and allows H-1B filing at any time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether universities, affiliated research institutions, or nonprofit research bodies in your field have relevant roles; explore concurrent employment structures with a lawyer

decision_factors.factor: Extraordinary ability evidence (for O-1 or EB-1A)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: O-1 and EB-1A bypass the H-1B lottery but require documented evidence of outstanding achievement; the threshold is substantially higher than H-1B
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Audit your profile: peer-reviewed publications and citation counts, awards, media coverage, peer review service, salary relative to field peers

decision_factors.why_it_matters: TN visa is available at the port of entry with no quota, making it a fast and reliable alternative for eligible professions
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm citizenship status and whether your occupation is on the USMCA/TN eligible profession list

decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each year of remaining STEM OPT equals one more lottery attempt; knowing how many attempts remain shapes the urgency of backup planning
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate graduation date and STEM OPT extension expiry; map to remaining April lottery windows

decision_factors.factor: Tolerance for Canada as an alternative destination
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is quota-free and points-based, with PGWP providing open work permit flexibility; many H-1B lottery failures have reached PR within 2-3 years
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate CRS score, assess provincial nominee program eligibility, compare lifestyle and career market factors

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Using a concurrent university appointment to maintain cap-exempt H-1B eligibility while working at a private company
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: The legal structure is valid but requires careful design by an immigration attorney; informal or poorly structured arrangements can be challenged and may jeopardize both the H-1B and employment
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: People who attempt this arrangement without legal counsel, or whose university role is not substantive enough to qualify

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Waiting through all three STEM OPT lottery attempts before exploring alternatives
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: If all three fail, the person exits OPT status with little or no preparation time for Canada PR, O-1 accumulation, or NIW filing — compressing a multi-year process into a crisis window
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Anyone who treats Plan B as a fallback rather than a parallel track

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Pursuing O-1 or EB-1A as a lottery alternative
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: These paths require years of documented achievement that cannot be manufactured on short notice; candidates who begin building this profile late in their OPT window are unlikely to qualify in time
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Candidates with strong but undocumented profiles who did not strategically accumulate evidence (publications, awards, peer review) early in their careers

failure_patterns.pattern: Treating the H-1B lottery as the only path and making no contingency plans during STEM OPT
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Optimism bias combined with the effort cost of researching alternatives; each lottery attempt feels like it might succeed, deferring backup planning indefinitely
failure_patterns.consequence: After three misses, the person is in a status crisis with no prepared alternatives, forcing rushed or suboptimal decisions (returning home, leaving the field, accepting worse roles)
warning_signs: Person is in second or third OPT year and has not researched O-1, NIW, or Canada PR
Person frames the question as 'what do I do after three misses' rather than 'what am I building in parallel'

diagnostic_questions.question: How many H-1B lottery attempts do you have remaining on STEM OPT?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines urgency and which paths are still viable within the current status window
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 1-2 remain: assess whether parallel-tracking Canada PR or NIW filing now is feasible alongside remaining attempts
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If zero remain: pivot immediately to cap-exempt employer, O-1, NIW, or Canada; no more time to defer

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have documented evidence of extraordinary ability (citations, awards, media, peer review, high relative salary)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines O-1 and EB-1A eligibility, which are the most powerful lottery-independent US paths
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Consult an immigration attorney about O-1 or EB-1A; these can move quickly if evidence is strong
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do not count on these paths in the short term; prioritize cap-exempt employer, NIW, or Canada instead

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you a Canadian or Mexican citizen?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: TN visa eligibility, which is quota-free and obtainable at the port of entry for eligible professions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Verify your occupation is on the TN eligible list and whether your employer qualifies; TN may be faster than any other path
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: TN is not available; assess other paths

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your employer or a potential employer affiliated with a university, nonprofit research institution, or government research agency?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Cap-exempt H-1B eligibility, which removes the lottery entirely
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Work with a lawyer to structure the employment relationship correctly to qualify for cap-exempt filing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Cap-exempt path is not directly available; explore concurrent appointment structures or transition to a cap-exempt employer

industry_realities.common_belief: If you miss H-1B three times, you have no real option but to leave the US
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple legitimate US paths exist: cap-exempt employer H-1B (no lottery), O-1 (no lottery), EB-1A (direct green card, no lottery), EB-2 NIW (self-petition green card, no lottery); Canada PR is also a viable parallel track with transparent point-based selection
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should map their profile against these alternatives early rather than treating departure as the default outcome of lottery failure

industry_realities.common_belief: EB-2 NIW requires an exceptionally high bar that most STEM students cannot meet
industry_realities.actual_reality: Under the Matter of Dhanasar three-prong test, many STEM graduates can qualify — the threshold is described by informed commenters as lower than commonly assumed for candidates with research backgrounds
industry_realities.implication: STEM candidates should not self-screen out of NIW without actually consulting an attorney or reviewing Dhanasar criteria; many may qualify without realizing it

industry_realities.common_belief: Canada is a fallback or consolation option compared to staying in the US
industry_realities.actual_reality: Canada's Express Entry PR process is quota-free and points-based (transparent and predictable), PGWP provides open work authorization with no employer-tying anxiety, and many candidates reach PR within 2 years — outcomes that are structurally better than H-1B lottery dependence for some profiles
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should evaluate Canada as a primary strategic option, not only as a last resort after US paths are exhausted

junior_vs_senior_signals.dimension: How to think about H-1B lottery failure
junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Treat three lottery misses as the end of US career prospects; frame the question as 'what are my options now that everything has failed'
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Treat the lottery as one mechanism among several; begin building O-1/NIW evidence and evaluating Canada in parallel during OPT years so that lottery failure triggers a prepared transition rather than a crisis
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Waiting until after three misses compresses multi-year preparation requirements into a short crisis window, forcing worse outcomes

junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Assume H-1B always requires lottery and that the only way around it is to get lucky
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Actively research whether your employer or a concurrent appointment qualifies for cap-exempt H-1B; use attorney-designed concurrent employment structures if needed
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Cap-exempt employment entirely removes the lottery variable, but most candidates are unaware it exists or how to access it

community_misconceptions.misconception: H-1B is the only work visa that lets you stay long-term in the US after graduating
community_misconceptions.why_believed: H-1B is by far the most discussed visa in international student communities, creating a availability-bias illusion that it is the only path
community_misconceptions.actual_rule: O-1, TN (for Canadian/Mexican citizens), cap-exempt H-1B through qualifying employers, EB-1A, and EB-2 NIW are all legally viable long-term paths that do not depend on the H-1B lottery
community_misconceptions.real_consequence: Candidates who believe H-1B is the only path do not build the evidence or connections needed for alternatives, leaving them with no prepared options after lottery failure

community_misconceptions.misconception: EB-2 NIW is only for professors and senior researchers, not typical STEM graduates
community_misconceptions.why_believed: The name 'national interest waiver' sounds like a high bar requiring nationally recognized impact; community discourse tends to highlight extreme cases
community_misconceptions.actual_rule: The Matter of Dhanasar framework has three prongs that many STEM graduates with research backgrounds can satisfy; the bar is more accessible than the label implies
community_misconceptions.real_consequence: Eligible candidates self-screen out without consulting a lawyer, missing a path that could have led to a green card without employer sponsorship

real_case_outcomes.situation: A person in the US on STEM OPT used a university-company concurrent employment structure: part-time researcher at a university (cap-exempt), full-time at a private company
real_case_outcomes.decision: Structured the arrangement with an immigration attorney to qualify for cap-exempt H-1B sponsorship through the university affiliation
real_case_outcomes.outcome: Successfully obtained H-1B outside the lottery system through the cap-exempt university employer while maintaining private sector employment
real_case_outcomes.lesson: Concurrent employment between a cap-exempt and a cap-subject employer is legally valid but requires attorney-designed structure; it is a real and used path, not theoretical

real_case_outcomes.situation: Multiple H-1B lottery failures by Chinese and Indian international students
real_case_outcomes.decision: Redirected to Canada via Express Entry or employer-sponsored PGWP pathway
real_case_outcomes.outcome: Obtained Canadian PR within 2-3 years; gained open work permit status (PGWP) in the interim, eliminating employer-tying anxiety
real_case_outcomes.lesson: Canada PR is a structurally predictable alternative that many H-1B lottery failures have executed successfully within a 2-3 year window; it should be planned in parallel, not treated as a last resort

psychological_barriers.trigger: Framing the question as 'what options do I have after three lottery misses' rather than 'what should I be building now in case I miss'
psychological_barriers.underlying_state: Optimism bias during OPT years defers backup planning; when the worst case materializes, loss aversion and sunk-cost framing make alternatives feel like giving up rather than adapting
psychological_barriers.how_it_manifests: Person asks about alternatives only after all lottery attempts are exhausted, presenting a compressed timeline and fewer viable options
psychological_barriers.expert_move: Reframe the question: 'The 25-30% three-miss probability is not a tail risk — it's a planning scenario. What would you be doing differently today if you assumed you would miss all three?' This moves the person from reactive to proactive mode.

psychological_barriers.trigger: Describing Canada as a fallback or lesser option after US paths fail
psychological_barriers.underlying_state: Status quo bias and peer comparison anxiety — staying in the US is the socially legible success signal in Chinese international student communities; choosing Canada feels like admitting defeat
psychological_barriers.how_it_manifests: Person delays or dismisses Canada research despite objective advantages (no lottery, transparent PR path, open work permit), waiting until US options are exhausted
psychological_barriers.expert_move: Reframe Canada as a parallel strategic track: 'Many people who went to Canada are now PRs with job security, while their US peers are still on year 3 of H-1B uncertainty. Which outcome do you actually want?' Focus on outcomes, not on which country sounds more prestigious.

reframing_moves.original_framing: I missed H-1B three times — what are my legal options to stay in the US?
reframing_moves.reframe: The three-miss probability is 25-30%, not a rare edge case. The real question is: what should someone on OPT be building in parallel so that missing all three triggers a transition rather than a crisis?
reframing_moves.why_it_works: Shifts the person from reactive problem-solving (options after failure) to proactive risk management (preparation before failure), which is the only frame in which good outcomes are still achievable
reframing_moves.example_language: 做好这个心理预期，早早准备Plan B，不是认输，是理性规划 ('Building this expectation and preparing Plan B early is not giving up — it is rational planning')

reframing_moves.original_framing: Canada is a fallback if I can't make it work in the US
reframing_moves.reframe: Canada's immigration system is structurally superior for predictability: points-based, no lottery, transparent CRS score, PGWP open work permit. For many profiles it is not a consolation — it is the better-designed system.
reframing_moves.why_it_works: Removes the emotional hierarchy (US = success, Canada = failure) and replaces it with a systems comparison, allowing the person to evaluate the actual outcomes rather than the social signal
reframing_moves.example_language: 加拿大这条路值得认真研究，不是退而求其次 ('The Canada path deserves serious research — it is not a second-best option')
